Ranunculus, commonly known as the buttercup or spearwort, is a genus of over 600 species of flowering plants in the family Ranunculaceae. The health benefits of Ranunculus include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, and its potential to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress.

The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ranunculus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various conditions, including arthritis, skin conditions, and digestive issues.
